questions.text: The lithosphere is broken into a number of plates known as the:
questions.explanation: These plates move very slowly—just a few millimetres each year.

questions.text: The movement of lithospheric plates is caused by:
options: Movement of molten magma inside the earth
questions.explanation: The molten magma inside the earth moves in a circular manner.

questions.text: Forces which act in the interior of the earth are called:
questions.explanation: Endogenic forces sometimes produce sudden movements like earthquakes and volcanoes.

questions.text: Forces that work on the surface of the earth are called:
questions.explanation: Exogenic forces lead to erosion and deposition.

questions.text: A vent (opening) in the earth's crust through which molten material erupts suddenly is a:
questions.explanation: Magma from the mantle reaches the surface through a volcano.

questions.text: Vibrations produced when lithospheric plates move are called:
questions.explanation: These vibrations can travel all round the earth.

questions.text: The place in the crust where the earthquake movement starts is the:
questions.explanation: The focus is the point of origin of seismic energy.

questions.text: The place on the surface above the focus is called the:
questions.explanation: Greatest damage usually occurs closest to the epicentre.

questions.text: Earthquake vibrations are measured by a machine called a:
questions.explanation: It records the waves generated by an earthquake.

questions.text: The magnitude of an earthquake is measured on the:
questions.explanation: An earthquake of 2.0 or less can be felt only a little.

questions.text: An earthquake is classified as a 'major earthquake' if its Richter scale magnitude is:
questions.explanation: Earthquakes of 7.0 magnitude and above are very destructive.

questions.text: The wearing away of the landscape by different agents like water, wind and ice is:
questions.explanation: Eroded material is carried away and eventually deposited.

questions.text: As the river enters the plain it twists and turns forming large bends known as:
questions.explanation: Continuous erosion and deposition along the sides of the meander change its shape.

questions.text: A cutoff lake formed when a meander loop is cut off from the river is a/an:
questions.explanation: It looks like a bow used for oxen, hence the name.

questions.text: Raised banks of a river are called:
questions.explanation: They are formed by the deposition of fine soil during floods.

questions.text: The collection of sediments from all the mouths of a river forms a:
questions.explanation: Deltas are typically triangular landforms at the river's mouth.

questions.text: Hollow-like caves formed on sea cliffs by the action of waves are:
questions.explanation: Continuous striking of waves makes the cracks larger and wider.

questions.text: When only the roof of the sea caves remains, they are called:
questions.explanation: Eventually, even the roof is eroded, leaving only walls.

questions.text: Wall-like features left after the roof of sea arches collapses are:
questions.explanation: Stacks are isolated pillars of rock in the sea.

questions.text: The steep rocky coast rising almost vertically above sea water is the:
questions.explanation: It is formed by the constant erosive action of sea waves.

questions.text: Glaciers are 'rivers of ________'.
questions.explanation: Glaciers erode the landscape by bulldozing soil and stones.

questions.text: The material carried by the glacier such as rocks, sand and silt gets deposited to form:
questions.explanation: Moraines are characteristic depositional landforms of glaciers.

questions.text: An active agent of erosion and deposition in the deserts is:
questions.explanation: Wind can easily transport loose sand in arid regions.

questions.text: Rocks in deserts that have a narrow base and wide top are:
questions.explanation: Winds erode the lower section of the rock more than the upper part.

questions.text: When the wind blows, it lifts and transports sand. When it stops, the sand falls and gets deposited in low hill-like structures called:
questions.explanation: Sand dunes are common features in sandy deserts like the Thar.

questions.text: When very fine and light sand is deposited in large areas, it is called:
questions.explanation: Large deposits of loess are found in China.

questions.text: Which is NOT an erosional feature of sea waves?
questions.explanation: A beach is a depositional feature where waves deposit sediments along the shores.

questions.text: The highest waterfall in the world is Angel Falls located in:
questions.explanation: It is in South America.

questions.text: Which waterfall is located on the border between Canada and USA?
questions.explanation: Niagara is a world-famous group of three waterfalls.

questions.text: Sudden movements in the earth's crust cause:
questions.explanation: Endogenic forces are responsible for sudden geological events.

questions.text: Which of the following is an exogenic force?
questions.explanation: Rivers act on the surface of the earth to reshape it.

questions.text: Seismic waves are of how many types?
questions.explanation: They are P waves (longitudinal), S waves (transverse), and L waves (surface).

questions.text: Earthquake prediction is difficult, but some people use animal behavior like:
questions.explanation: Agitated fish and snakes coming to the surface are traditional local earthquake signs.

questions.text: In 2001, a massive earthquake of 6.9 magnitude hit ________ town in Gujarat.
questions.explanation: The Bhuj earthquake caused widespread destruction on Republic Day.

questions.text: The breaking up of the rocks on the earth's surface is:
questions.explanation: Weathering happens in situ (at the same place).

questions.text: The place where a river breaks into small streams called distributaries is its:
questions.explanation: As the river slows down near the sea, it cannot carry its load and splits.

questions.text: Beaches are formed by:
questions.explanation: Sediments like sand and gravel are piled up by waves.

questions.text: As ice melts, glaciers leave behind deep hollows which get filled with water and become:
questions.explanation: These are called glacial lakes or cirque lakes.

questions.text: Which feature is formed when a river falls at a steep angle over hard rocks?
questions.explanation: Waterfalls are spectacular features of a river's upper course.

questions.text: Earthquakes and volcanoes cause ________ changes on the earth's surface.
questions.explanation: They are catastrophic events that can change the landscape in minutes.
